Title: Create new loyalty program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, Loyalty module is accessible at #!/workspace/loyalty
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty
2. Click the 'Add' button in the toolbar
3. Fill in Name: 'Test Loyalty Program'
4. Select Store: '@td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)' from the dropdown
5. Set Priority: 1
6. Toggle 'Is active' to ON
7. Set Start date to today
8. Click 'Save'
Expected Result:
1. New loyalty program blade opens with empty fields
2. All fields accept input without errors
3. Program is saved successfully
4. Program appears in the loyalty programs list
5. Success notification is displayed

Title: View loyalty program details
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, at least one loyalty program exists (e.g. 'Completed order')
Steps: 1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty\n2. Click on the existing 'Completed order' program in the list
Expected Result:
1. Program detail blade opens
2. All fields are displayed: Is active (toggle), Priority, Name, Localized names, Store, Start date, End date
3. Conditions section shows configured conditions
4. Rewards section shows configured rewards
5. All values match what was previously saved

Title: Edit existing loyalty program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, at least one loyalty program exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty
2. Click on an existing program to open details
3. Change Name to 'Updated Program Name'
4. Change Priority from current value to 10
5. Toggle 'Is active' to the opposite state
6. Click 'Save'
7. Close the blade and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. All fields are editable
2. Save completes without errors
3. Reopened program shows all updated values persisted correctly
4. Name, Priority, and active state reflect the changes

Title: Delete loyalty program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a test loyalty program exists that can be deleted
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty
2. Select a test program from the list
3. Click the 'Delete' button in the toolbar
4. Confirm the deletion in the confirmation dialog
Expected Result:
1. Confirmation dialog appears before deletion
2. After confirmation, the program is removed from the list
3. The program no longer appears in search results
4. No orphaned data remains (conditions/rewards cleaned up)

Title: Add single condition to program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty
2. Open an existing program or create a new one
3. In the Conditions section ('If any of the following criteria'), click 'Add condition'
4. Select condition type: 'Order status'
5. Set value to 'Completed'
6. Click 'Save'
Expected Result:
1. Condition builder UI appears after clicking 'Add condition'
2. Condition type dropdown lists available condition types
3. Condition is added and displayed in the conditions list
4. Save completes successfully
5. Reopening the program shows the condition persisted

Title: Add multiple conditions to program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Click 'Add condition' and configure first condition (e.g. Order status is Completed)
3. Click 'Add condition' again and configure a second condition
4. Click 'Save'
5. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Multiple conditions can be added to the same program
2. All conditions are displayed in the conditions section
3. The logical operator 'If any of the following criteria' applies to all conditions
4. Both conditions persist after save and reopen

Title: Remove condition from program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program exists with at least one condition configured
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program that has conditions
2. Locate an existing condition in the conditions list
3. Remove the condition (click delete/remove button on the condition row)
4. Click 'Save'
5. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Condition is removed from the conditions list in the UI
2. Save completes without errors
3. Reopened program no longer shows the removed condition
4. Other conditions (if any) remain unaffected

Title: Condition with different order statuses
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Add a condition with Order status = 'Completed'
3. Save and verify
4. Edit the condition to change status to a different available value (e.g. 'Paid', 'Shipped')
5. Save and verify
Expected Result:
1. Order status dropdown lists all available order statuses
2. Each status value can be selected and saved
3. The condition correctly reflects the updated status after save
4. Program functions correctly with different status values

Title: Set fixed points reward
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. In the Rewards section, click 'Add reward'
3. Set reward type to fixed points
4. Enter points value: 5.00
5. Click 'Save'
6. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Reward builder UI appears after clicking 'Add reward'
2. Points value field accepts decimal input
3. Reward is displayed as 'Get 5.00 fixed points'
4. Save completes successfully
5. Reward persists correctly after reopen

Title: Modify reward amount
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program exists with a reward configured (e.g. 5.00 fixed points)
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program with an existing reward
2. Change the points value from 5.00 to 15.50
3. Click 'Save'
4. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Points value field is editable
2. New value 15.50 is accepted
3. Save completes without errors
4. Reopened program shows the updated reward value of 15.50

Title: Add multiple rewards to program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Click 'Add reward' and set first reward to 5.00 fixed points
3. Click 'Add reward' again and set second reward to 10.00 fixed points
4. Click 'Save'
5. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Multiple rewards can be added to the same program
2. Each reward is displayed separately in the rewards section
3. All rewards persist after save
4. Reopened program shows both rewards with correct values

Title: Toggle program active/inactive
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program exists
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Note the current state of the 'Is active' toggle
3. Toggle 'Is active' to OFF (inactive)
4. Click 'Save'
5. Verify in the list view that the program status reflects inactive
6. Reopen and toggle 'Is active' back to ON
7. Save and verify
Expected Result:
1. Toggle switches between active and inactive states
2. Save persists the toggle state
3. List view reflects the current active/inactive status
4. Toggling back to active works correctly
5. No data loss occurs when toggling status

Title: Start and end date scheduling
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Set Start date to a future date (e.g. tomorrow)
3. Set End date to a date after the start date (e.g. next month)
4. Click 'Save'
5. Close and reopen the program
6. Verify both dates are persisted
7. Try setting End date before Start date and save
Expected Result:
1. Date pickers allow selecting dates
2. Both start and end dates persist correctly after save
3. Dates are displayed in the correct format
4. Setting End date before Start date should either show a validation error or be prevented

Title: Priority ordering between programs
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, at least two loyalty programs exist
Steps:
1. Open the first loyalty program and set Priority to 1
2. Save and close
3. Open the second loyalty program and set Priority to 2
4. Save and close
5. View the loyalty programs list
6. Verify the ordering reflects priority values
Expected Result:
1. Priority field accepts integer values
2. Each program can have a different priority value
3. Programs list respects priority ordering
4. Changing priority and re-saving updates the order correctly

Title: Add localized name for program
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Navigate to the Localized names section
3. Add a localized name for 'es-ES': 'Programa de Lealtad'
4. Add a localized name for 'pl-PL': 'Program Lojalnosciowy'
5. Click 'Save'
6. Close and reopen the program
Expected Result:
1. Localized names section is accessible
2. Language options include es-ES and pl-PL
3. Text input accepts Unicode characters
4. Both localized names persist after save
5. Reopened program shows correct localized values for each language

Title: Verify all language options available
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. Navigate to the Localized names section
3. Expand the language selection dropdown or list
4. Document all available language options
Expected Result:
1. Language options include at minimum: es-ES, pl-PL
2. All 13 platform-supported languages are available (EN, DE, FR, ES, NO, SV, PL, IT, PT, JA, ZH, FI, RU)
3. Each language can be selected and a localized name entered
4. No duplicate language entries exist

Title: Create program without required name
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, Loyalty module is accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to #!/workspace/loyalty
2. Click the 'Add' button
3. Leave the Name field empty
4. Fill in other fields (Store, Priority, dates)
5. Click 'Save'
Expected Result:
1. Validation error is displayed indicating Name is required
2. Program is NOT saved
3. No empty-named program appears in the list
4. Error message is clear and user-friendly
5. No server error (500) occurs - validation is handled gracefully

Title: Set negative points value for reward
Preconditions: Admin user is logged in, a loyalty program is open for editing
Steps:
1. Open a loyalty program
2. In the Rewards section, add or edit a reward
3. Enter a negative points value: -5.00
4. Click 'Save'
Expected Result:
1. Either the input field prevents negative values (client-side validation) or a validation error is displayed on save
2. Negative points reward is NOT saved to the program
3. No server error (500) occurs
4. Error message clearly indicates points must be a positive value
